title: haloragis teucrioides
intro: Haloragis teucrioides, also known as water milfoil or water-dragon, is a plant species native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its aquatic habit and distinctive leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are beneficial for treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Haloragis teucrioides include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and aid in the healing process of wounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in topical applications, such as salves or creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
